evidence: The paper applies random forest variable importance only as one intermediate dimensionality reduction method ("pRF = Pearson correlation-based redundancy reduction with random survival forest variable importance"), tested alongside hierarchical clustering, RIDGE, and no reduction. Model selection is based on held-out C-index: "We selected the radiomics and combined models yielding the highest average C-index per each combination of study cohort...for further evaluation." Conclusions address predictive performance, not feature importance ranking: "Pre-treatment PET/CT radiomics biomarkers may provide complementary prognostic value...beyond AJCC/UICC staging." Validation is robust (33 repeats of threefold cross-validation with proper information-leakage prevention, corrected paired t-tests), and they never report or interpret individual feature importance magnitudes or rankings.
